info.name: Armada API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Armada multi-agent orchestration system.

Version: 0.7.0

## Setup
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your Admiral server (default: `http://localhost:7890`)
2. For bearer token auth, set `bearerToken` (default: `default`)
3. For session token auth, use the Authenticate request and set `sessionToken` from the response
4. Legacy: If API key authentication is enabled, set the `apiKey` variable (deprecated)
5. Use the Status > Health Check request to verify connectivity

## Role model
- IsAdmin=true: global system-wide admin
- IsAdmin=false and IsTenantAdmin=true: tenant-scoped admin
- IsAdmin=false and IsTenantAdmin=false: regular user with self-service on own account and credentials

## Notes
- Tenant, user, and credential protection is server-controlled through IsProtected
- Immutable fields such as IDs, ownership columns, and creation timestamps are preserved server-side on update
- Includes Objectives, Delivery workflow profiles, structured checks, environments, deployments, incidents, runbooks, releases, cross-entity history, Workspace, Planning Sessions, Request History, Mux runtime helpers, and OpenAPI discovery examples.

variable.description: Planning session ID captured from the Planning Sessions folder.
Request history entry ID for detail/delete calls.
Repository-relative workspace path using forward slashes.
Mux endpoint name for runtime helper requests.
Optional Mux config directory override. Leave blank to use the default Mux config.
Workflow profile ID for delivery profile requests.
Check run ID for detail, retry, and delete requests.
Release ID for detail, refresh, update, and delete requests.
Objective ID for detail, update, and delete requests.
Environment ID for detail, update, and delete requests.
Deployment ID for detail and bounded action requests.
Incident ID for detail, update, and delete requests.
Runbook ID for detail, update, delete, and execution requests.
Runbook execution ID for detail, update, and delete requests.

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Authentication and identity endpoints. These endpoints handle login, identity lookup, and self-registration.

request.description: Look up which tenants a given email address belongs to. No authentication required. Used by the dashboard login flow.
Authenticate with tenant ID, email, and password. Returns an encrypted session token (24-hour lifetime). The test script auto-saves the token to the sessionToken variable.
Returns the authenticated user's tenant and user information. Requires authentication via bearer token or session token.

response.name: Identity
response.body:
{
  "Tenant": {
    "Id": "default",
    "Name": "Default Tenant",
    "Active": true,
    "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z",
    "LastUpdateUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
  },
  "User": {
    "Id": "default",
    "TenantId": "default",
    "Email": "admin@armada",
    "PasswordSha256": "********",
    "FirstName": null,
    "LastName": null,
    "IsAdmin": true,
    "IsTenantAdmin": true,
    "Active": true,
    "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z",
    "LastUpdateUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
  }
}

request.description: Self-register a new user within an existing tenant. Creates a user and bearer token credential. Requires AllowSelfRegistration to be enabled in settings.

item.name: Tenants
item.description: Tenant management endpoints. Global admin only for list/create/update/delete. Authenticated non-admin users may read only their own tenant by ID.

request.description: List all tenants (paginated). Global admin only.
Enumerate tenants with filtering and sorting. Global admin only.
Create a new tenant. Global admin only. The server seeds a protected admin@armada user and default credential for the new tenant. The seeded user is tenant admin only.
Get a tenant by ID. Global admin: any tenant. Tenant admin or regular user: own tenant only.
Update a tenant. Global admin only. Immutable fields such as Id, CreatedUtc, LastUpdateUtc, and IsProtected are server-controlled.
Delete a tenant. Global admin only. Returns 403 for protected tenants. Deleting an unprotected tenant cascades to all subordinate tenant resources, including protected child auth records.

item.name: Users
item.description: User management endpoints. Global admins can manage all users. Tenant admins can manage users within their tenant. Regular users can read and update only themselves.

request.description: List users (paginated). Global admins can list all users. Tenant admins can list users in their own tenant. Passwords are redacted.
Enumerate users with filtering and sorting. Global admins can enumerate all users. Tenant admins are limited to their own tenant.

Create a new user. Global admins can create users anywhere. Tenant admins can create users only in their tenant and cannot grant global admin. Password is plaintext in the request body and is hashed server-side. PasswordSha256 remains accepted for backward compatibility. IsProtected is server-controlled.
Get a user by ID. Global admin: any user. Tenant admin: users in own tenant. Regular user: own user only. Password is redacted.

Update a user. Global admins can update any user. Tenant admins can update users in their tenant. Regular users can update only themselves. If Password is supplied, the server hashes and stores the new password; if omitted, the existing password is preserved. Immutable fields such as Id, TenantId, CreatedUtc, LastUpdateUtc, and IsProtected are server-controlled.
Delete a user. Global admins can delete any unprotected user. Tenant admins can delete unprotected users in their tenant. Regular users cannot delete users directly. Returns 403 for protected users.

item.name: Credentials
item.description: Credential (bearer token) management endpoints. Admins can manage all; non-admins can manage their own.

request.description: List credentials (paginated). Global admin: all. Tenant admin: credentials in own tenant. Regular user: own credentials only.
Enumerate credentials with filtering. Results are scoped by role: global admin all, tenant admin own tenant, regular user own only.
Create a new credential with auto-generated bearer token. Global admin: any tenant/user. Tenant admin: users in own tenant. Regular user: self only. IsProtected is server-controlled.
Get a credential by ID. Global admin: any. Tenant admin: credentials in own tenant. Regular user: own only.

Update a credential. Global admins can update any credential. Tenant admins can update credentials in their tenant. Regular users can update only their own credentials. Immutable fields such as Id, TenantId, UserId, CreatedUtc, LastUpdateUtc, and IsProtected are server-controlled.
Delete a credential. Global admin: any. Tenant admin: credentials in own tenant. Regular user: own only. Returns 403 for protected credentials.

item.name: Status
item.description: Health check and system status endpoints.

request.description: Returns health status, uptime, version, port configuration, and remote tunnel telemetry. Does not require authentication.
Returns aggregate status including captain counts, mission breakdown by status, active voyages with progress, recent signals, and remote tunnel state.
Returns current server settings including remote control configuration.

response.name: Settings
response.body:
{
  "AdmiralPort": 7890,
  "McpPort": 7891,
  "MaxCaptains": 0,
  "HeartbeatIntervalSeconds": 30,
  "StallThresholdMinutes": 10,
  "IdleCaptainTimeoutSeconds": 0,
  "AutoCreatePr": false,
  "DataDirectory": "C:\\Users\\joelc\\.armada",
  "DatabasePath": "C:\\Users\\joelc\\.armada\\armada.db",
  "LogDirectory": "C:\\Users\\joelc\\.armada\\logs",
  "DocksDirectory": "C:\\Users\\joelc\\.armada\\docks",
  "ReposDirectory": "C:\\Users\\joelc\\.armada\\repos",
  "RemoteControl": {
    "Enabled": false,
    "TunnelUrl": null,
    "InstanceId": null,
    "EnrollmentToken": null,
    "ConnectTimeoutSeconds": 15,
    "HeartbeatIntervalSeconds": 30,
    "ReconnectBaseDelaySeconds": 5,
    "ReconnectMaxDelaySeconds": 60,
    "AllowInvalidCertificates": false
  }
}

request.description: Updates editable server settings. When RemoteControl is supplied it replaces the full remoteControl object.
Initiates a graceful shutdown of the Admiral server. The server will stop after a brief delay.

item.name: System
item.description: System-level helper surfaces including OpenAPI discovery, request history, and runtime endpoint discovery.

request.description: Download the live OpenAPI document used by Armada's API Explorer.
Open the interactive Swagger UI for the live REST surface.
List captured request-history entries scoped to the authenticated caller.
Return aggregate request-history counts and time buckets.
Read one captured request with expanded headers, params, and body snapshots.
Delete one request-history entry. Tenant admin or global admin only.
Delete multiple request-history entries by ID. Tenant admin or global admin only.
Delete all request-history entries matching the supplied filters within the caller's scope. Tenant admin or global admin only.
List saved Mux endpoints. Add the optional configDirectory query parameter to inspect a non-default Mux config directory.
Inspect one saved Mux endpoint with redacted secrets. Add the optional configDirectory query parameter to inspect a non-default Mux config directory.

item.name: History
item.description: Cross-entity delivery timeline queries and reporting.

query.description: Optional comma-separated source types such as CheckRun, Release, Voyage, Mission

request.description: List the cross-entity Armada timeline for the current scope.
Enumerate the cross-entity Armada timeline using a JSON body.

item.name: Fleets
item.description: Fleet (repository collection) management. A fleet groups related vessels (repositories) together.

request.description: Returns all registered fleets with pagination.
Paginated enumeration of fleets with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body. Query string parameters override body values.
Creates a new fleet and returns it with an assigned ID (flt_ prefix). Auto-saves the ID to the fleetId collection variable.
Returns a single fleet by ID.
Updates an existing fleet. The request body is merged with the existing fleet.
Deletes a fleet. Vessels in the fleet are not deleted; their FleetId is set to null.
Batch delete multiple fleets by ID. Returns a summary of deleted and skipped entries.

item.name: Vessels
item.description: Vessel (git repository) management. A vessel represents a single git repository registered with Armada.

request.description: Returns all registered vessels with pagination. Optionally filter by fleetId.
Paginated enumeration of vessels with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Registers a new vessel (git repository). Auto-saves the ID to the vesselId collection variable.
Returns a single vessel by ID.
Updates an existing vessel.
Deletes a vessel.
Batch delete multiple vessels by ID. Returns a summary of deleted and skipped entries.

item.name: Workspace
item.description: First-class vessel workspace browsing, editing, search, git-aware status, and file operations.

request.description: List the root directory of the vessel workspace.
Read one workspace file by repository-relative path.
Save one text file in the vessel workspace with optimistic concurrency support.
Create a directory inside the vessel workspace.
Rename or move one workspace file or directory.
Delete one workspace file or directory.
Search text files in the vessel workspace.
Return branch state and changed files for the vessel workspace.
Return high-level workspace status, git state, and active mission overlap context.

item.name: Voyages
item.description: Voyage (mission batch) management. A voyage groups related missions and tracks their collective progress.

request.description: Returns all voyages with pagination. Optionally filter by status.
Paginated enumeration of voyages with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.

Creates a new voyage with optional missions and ordered playbook selections. Each selected playbook includes its own DeliveryMode: InlineFullContent, InstructionWithReference, or AttachIntoWorktree. Missions are automatically dispatched to the target vessel. Auto-saves the ID to the voyageId collection variable.

response.name: Created

request.description: Returns a voyage and all its associated missions.
Cancels a voyage and all its Pending/Assigned missions. In-progress missions are not affected.
Permanently deletes a voyage and all its associated missions from the database. This cannot be undone.
Batch delete multiple voyages and their associated missions by ID. Voyages that are Open/InProgress or have active missions are skipped.

item.name: Playbooks
item.description: Tenant-scoped markdown playbook management. Playbooks are reusable instruction documents that can be selected during voyage and mission dispatch.

request.description: Returns tenant-scoped playbooks with pagination.
Paginated enumeration of playbooks with JSON query body.

body.raw:
{
  "FileName": "CSHARP_BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE.md",
  "Description": "Backend architecture and implementation rules for C# services.",
  "Content": "# C# Backend Architecture

## Requirements
- Use dependency injection
- Prefer async APIs
- Add tests for externally visible behavior
",
  "Active": true
}

request.description: Creates a tenant-scoped markdown playbook. Auto-saves the ID to the playbookId collection variable.
Returns a single playbook by ID.

body.raw:
{
  "FileName": "CSHARP_BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE.md",
  "Description": "Updated architecture rules for C# backend missions.",
  "Content": "# C# Backend Architecture

## Requirements
- Use dependency injection
- Prefer async APIs
- Keep controllers thin
",
  "Active": true
}

request.description: Updates the file name, description, content, or active state of a playbook.
Deletes a playbook. Existing mission snapshots remain immutable.

item.name: Missions
item.description: Mission (atomic work unit) management. A mission is a single task assigned to an AI captain agent.

query.description: Filter: Pending, Assigned, InProgress, Testing, Review, Complete, Failed, Cancelled

request.description: Returns all missions with pagination. Supports filtering by status, vesselId, captainId, and voyageId.
Paginated enumeration of missions with full filtering support via JSON body.
Creates and dispatches a new mission. If a VesselId is provided, the Admiral will assign a captain and set up a worktree. Auto-saves the ID to the missionId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "msn_abc123",
  "VoyageId": null,
  "VesselId": "vsl_abc123",
  "CaptainId": null,
  "Title": "Fix login bug",
  "Description": "The login form does not validate email addresses.",
  "Status": "Pending",
  "Priority": 100,
  "BranchName": null,
  "PrUrl": null,
  "TotalRuntimeMs": null,
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description: Returns a single mission by ID.
Updates mission fields (title, description, priority). Does not change status -- use the status transition endpoint for that.

Transitions a mission to a new status.

Valid transitions:
- Pending -> Assigned, Cancelled
- Assigned -> InProgress, Cancelled
- InProgress -> Testing, Review, Complete, Failed, Cancelled
- Testing -> Review, InProgress, Complete, Failed
- Review -> Complete, InProgress, Failed
- Complete, Failed, Cancelled are terminal states
Transition from Assigned to InProgress.
Transition from InProgress to Complete.
Cancels a mission by setting its status to Cancelled.
Restarts a failed or cancelled mission by resetting it to Pending. Optionally update title and description before restarting. Body is optional.
Returns the git diff of changes made by a captain in the mission's worktree. Checks for a saved diff first, then falls back to a live worktree diff.
Returns the session log for a mission. Supports pagination via ?lines=N and ?offset=N query parameters.
Batch delete multiple missions by ID. Returns a summary of deleted and skipped entries.

item.name: Captains
item.description: Captain (AI agent) management. A captain is an AI agent instance that executes missions.

query.description: Filter by state: Idle, Working, Stalled, Stopping

request.description: Returns all captains with pagination. The 'status' filter maps to the captain's State field.
Paginated enumeration of captains with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.

Registers a new captain (AI agent). Runtime options: ClaudeCode, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Custom. Model is optional; when omitted, the runtime chooses automatically. SystemInstructions are optional per-captain instructions injected into every mission prompt. Invalid or unavailable models return a validation error. Auto-saves the ID to the captainId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "cpt_abc123",
  "Name": "captain-1",
  "Runtime": "ClaudeCode",
  "Model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "SystemInstructions": "You are a testing specialist. Always run tests before committing.",
  "State": "Idle",
  "CurrentMissionId": null,
  "CurrentDockId": null,
  "ProcessId": null,
  "RecoveryAttempts": 0,
  "LastHeartbeatUtc": null,
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z",
  "LastUpdateUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description: Returns a single captain by ID.
Updates a captain's name, runtime, or model. Operational fields (state, process, mission) are preserved. Invalid or unavailable models return a validation error.
Stops a running captain agent. Kills its OS process and recalls it to idle state.
Returns the current session log for a captain, resolved via the .current pointer file. Supports pagination via ?lines=N and ?offset=N.

response.name: Log Available

request.description: Deletes a captain. If the captain is currently working, it is recalled first.
Batch delete multiple captains by ID. Captains that are Working or have active missions are skipped.

item.name: Planning Sessions
item.description: Planning-session lifecycle, transcript turns, summarize-to-dispatch, and direct dispatch flows.

request.description: List planning sessions visible to the authenticated caller.
Create a planning session, reserve the selected captain, and provision a planning dock.
Read one planning session with transcript, captain, and vessel context.
Append one user message and launch the next planning turn.
Generate a dispatch-ready draft without launching the voyage.
Launch a voyage directly from planning output.
Stop an active planning session and release its captain and dock.
Delete a planning session and its transcript. Active sessions are stopped first.

item.name: Signals
item.description: Signal (inter-agent messaging) management. Signals are messages between the admiral and captains.

query.description: Filter: Assignment, Progress, Completion, Error, Heartbeat, Nudge, Mail

request.description: Returns signals with pagination. Supports filtering by signalType, toCaptainId, and unreadOnly.
Paginated enumeration of signals with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Sends a new signal (message). Type options: Assignment, Progress, Completion, Error, Heartbeat, Nudge, Mail. Auto-saves the ID to the signalId collection variable.

response.name: Created
response.body:
{
  "Id": "sig_abc123",
  "FromCaptainId": null,
  "ToCaptainId": "cpt_abc123",
  "Type": "Mail",
  "Payload": "Please check the test results and fix any failures.",
  "Read": false,
  "CreatedUtc": "2026-03-07T12:00:00Z"
}

request.description: Batch soft-delete multiple signals by marking them as read.

item.name: Events
item.description: System event log. Events are generated automatically by the server when state changes occur.

query.description: Filter by event type (e.g. mission.status_changed, captain.launched)

request.description: Returns system events with pagination. Supports filtering by type, captainId, missionId, vesselId, and voyageId. The 'limit' parameter is an alias for pageSize.
Paginated enumeration of events with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Delete a single event by ID.
Batch delete multiple events by ID.

item.name: Merge Queue
item.description: Bors-style merge queue with batch testing. Branches are queued, tested in batches, and passing batches are landed into the target branch.

request.description: Returns all entries in the merge queue with pagination.
Paginated enumeration of merge queue entries with optional filtering and sorting via JSON body.
Adds a branch to the merge queue for testing and merging. Auto-saves the ID to the mergeEntryId collection variable.
Returns a single merge queue entry by ID.
Cancels a queued merge entry by ID.
Triggers processing of all queued entries. Creates integration branches, runs tests, and lands passing batches.
Permanently deletes a terminal merge queue entry. Only Landed, Failed, or Cancelled entries can be purged.
Batch purge multiple terminal merge queue entries by ID. Returns a summary of purged and skipped entries.

item.name: Docks
item.description: Git worktree management for captains.

request.description: Returns a single dock by ID.
Deletes a dock and cleans up its git worktree. Blocked if the dock is actively in use.
Force purges a dock and its git worktree, even if a mission references it.
Batch delete multiple docks and their git worktrees by ID.

item.name: Delivery
item.description: Workflow profiles, structured check runs, and release records.

item.name: Workflow Profiles
item.description: Workflow profile CRUD, validation, and resolved-command preview flows.

query.description: Optional Global, Fleet, or Vessel scope filter

request.description: List workflow profiles visible to the authenticated caller.
Create a workflow profile for build/test/release/deploy command resolution. Saves the new ID to workflowProfileId.
Read one workflow profile by ID.
Update an existing workflow profile.
Validate a workflow profile definition without saving it.
Preview the resolved workflow commands and check types for one vessel.
Delete the current workflow profile.

item.name: Checks
item.description: Structured check-run execution, import, retry, and inspection.

request.description: List structured check runs in scope.
Execute a structured check run. Saves the new ID to checkRunId.
Import an externally executed check run into Armada history. Saves the new ID to checkRunId.
Read one structured check run by ID.
Retry a prior structured check run. Saves the new retry ID to checkRunId.
Delete one structured check run.

item.name: Releases
item.description: Release drafting, refresh, update, and inspection.

request.description: List release records in scope.
Create a release record from linked check runs, missions, and voyages. Saves the new ID to releaseId.
Read one release record by ID.
Update an existing release record.
Refresh derived release fields such as artifact aggregation and linked scope.
Delete one release record.

item.name: Workflows
item.description: Pre-built request sequences demonstrating common Armada workflows. Run these in order.

request.description: Step 1: Create a fleet to group repositories.
Step 2: Register a git repository as a vessel in the fleet.
Step 3: Register an AI captain agent.
Step 4: Create a voyage with multiple missions targeting the registered vessel.
Step 5: Check the system status to see the voyage and missions in progress.
Step 6: View the voyage detail with all its missions and their statuses.
Step 7: List missions for the voyage to see individual progress.
Step 8: View the event audit trail for all activity related to this voyage.

item.name: Proxy
item.description: Remote-control proxy endpoints.

item.name: Shell & Inspection
item.description: Read-oriented proxy endpoints and live inspection routes.

request.description: Returns proxy health and connected-instance counts.
Returns summary rows for known Armada instances.
Returns current summary and recent forwarded events for an instance.

request.description: Sends a live tunnel request for armada.status.snapshot.
Sends a live tunnel request for armada.status.health.

request.description: Returns the aggregated remote-operations shell summary for a connected instance.
Returns recent Armada activity rows for the selected instance.
Returns recently updated missions for the selected instance.
Returns recently updated voyages for the selected instance.
Returns recently updated captains for the selected instance.
Returns focused mission detail including related captain, voyage, vessel, and dock when available.
Returns a paged mission log slice.
Returns a saved or live mission diff for the selected mission.
Returns voyage detail and the associated mission chain.
Returns captain detail including current mission/dock and recent mission history.
Returns a paged captain log slice.

item.name: Management
item.description: Write-oriented proxy endpoints for fleets, vessels, voyages, missions, and captain control.

request.description: List fleets on the connected Armada instance through the proxy.
Get fleet detail from the connected Armada instance.
Create a fleet on the connected Armada instance.
Update an existing fleet through the proxy.
List vessels on the connected Armada instance through the proxy.
Get vessel detail from the connected Armada instance.
Create a vessel on the connected Armada instance.
Update a vessel through the proxy.
List voyages on the connected Armada instance through the proxy.
Dispatch a voyage on the connected Armada instance.
Cancel an active or pending voyage through the proxy.
List missions on the connected Armada instance through the proxy.
Create a mission through the proxy.

request.description: Update a mission through the proxy.

body.raw:

Restart a mission through the proxy.

item.name: Objectives
item.description: Internal-first intake and scope records linked to vessels, releases, deployments, and incidents.

request.description: List objective records in scope.
Create a scoped objective record and save the resulting ID to objectiveId.

body.raw:
{
  "Title": "Stabilize May rollout",
  "Description": "Track the rollout objective across API and dashboard vessels.",
  "Status": "Scoped",
  "Owner": "Delivery",
  "AcceptanceCriteria": ["Deployment verified in staging"],
  "VesselIds": ["{{vesselId}}"]
}

request.description: Read one objective by ID.
Update one objective record.
Delete one objective record.

item.name: Environments
item.description: Named rollout targets with approval, verification, and monitoring settings.

request.description: List environments in scope.
Create an environment and save the resulting ID to environmentId.
Read one environment by ID.
Update one environment.
Delete one environment record.

item.name: Deployments
item.description: Bounded deployment records with approval, verification, rollback, and linked evidence.

request.description: List deployments in scope.
Create a deployment and save the resulting ID to deploymentId.
Read one deployment by ID.
Update one deployment.
Approve one pending deployment.
Deny one pending deployment.
Run post-deploy verification for one deployment.
Run the configured rollback flow for one deployment.
Delete one deployment record.

item.name: Incidents
item.description: Operational incidents linked to environments, deployments, releases, and hotfix work.

request.description: List incidents in scope.
Create an incident and save the resulting ID to incidentId.
Read one incident by ID.
Update one incident record.
Delete one incident record.

item.name: Runbooks
item.description: Playbook-backed runbooks and event-backed execution history.

request.description: List runbooks in scope.
Create a runbook and save the resulting ID to runbookId.

body.raw:
{
  "Title": "Staging verification",
  "Description": "Run post-deploy verification steps.",
  "EnvironmentId": "{{environmentId}}",
  "DefaultCheckType": "DeploymentVerification",
  "Markdown": "# Staging verification

}
